title: Security Audit — 2026-05-18
summary: Strong foundational posture (bcrypt 12, parameterised pgx, CSRF double-submit, comprehensive security headers, rate limiting); 3 High findings — hardcoded dev/prod secrets in .env files and dangerouslySetInnerHTML on help-doc rendering — plus operational gaps around dev-endpoint auth and JWT rotation.
content:
Contents
  1. Executive Summary
  2. Findings Table
  3. Remediation Priorities
  4. Passed Checks
  5. Coverage Notes

Executive Summary

The MMFFDev Vector codebase demonstrates a mature security posture with strong fundamentals in authentication, password hashing, CSRF protection, and secure-by-default configurations. The development team has clearly invested in defense-in-depth across both the Next.js frontend and Go backend. However, several Medium to High severity findings require remediation, primarily around hardcoded development credentials exposed in version-controlled files, overly permissive XSS vulnerabilities via dangerouslySetInnerHTML, and production database hardlock procedures that could mask operational issues. The codebase follows OWASP best practices for parameterised queries, JWT validation, rate limiting, and security headers.

Top 5 Highest-Risk Findings

  1. Critical — Development database credentials and JWT secrets hardcoded in .env.local and .env.dev (git-ignored but still a risk).
  2. High — Unvalidated HTML rendering in help documentation via dangerouslySetInnerHTML in Header.tsx and HelpDocRenderer.tsx.
  3. High — Production database credentials in .env.production.locked accessible to operators (though hardlocked).
  4. High — Console logging of user session context in ScopeContext.tsx may leak grant/permission details in production.
  5. Medium — X-Forwarded-For header trust CIDR configuration must be explicitly set; empty default is safe but requires documentation and operator awareness.

Findings Table

FileLineSeverityCategoryFindingRecommendation
backend/.env.local8, 18, 20-21, 41-51, 59, 62, 72HighData SecurityDevelopment database password, JWT access/refresh secrets, master key, third-party credentials (Planka, CI service token) hardcoded in plaintext. File is git-ignored but if committed accidentally or shared, exposes all secrets.Move all secrets to a secrets manager (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, 1Password). Store only encrypted ENC[...] references per the pattern in .env.example. Use go run ./cmd/encsecret to encrypt.
backend/.env.dev8-10, 12-13, 18, 20-21, 33, 41, 46-47, 50-55, 59, 62HighData SecuritySame as .env.local: unencrypted development secrets (DB password, JWT secrets, API tokens, email creds). Sensitive even for dev — leaked credentials could be replayed.Same mitigation: encrypt or use secrets manager. Treat .env.dev secrets as encrypted by default.
backend/.env.production.locked1-27HighData SecurityProduction credentials hardlocked to __LOCKED__ placeholders, but original file containing real credentials sits on disk. No deletion or rotation policy documented.Add documented quarterly rotation. Move .env.production.locked off-repository. Verify no prod DB password exists in git history: git log -p -- '*env.production*'.
app/components/Header.tsx197HighXSSHelp documentation body HTML rendered via dangerouslySetInnerHTML from backend. Backend sanitisation mentioned in comments but not enforced; a buggy sanitiser or new attack vector could leak XSS.Verify sanitisation at write time with OWASP HTML Purifier or equivalent. Add CSP script-src 'none' on these pages. Add per-document CSP nonce.
app/components/HelpDocRenderer.tsx139HighXSSBody HTML from backend rendered via dangerouslySetInnerHTML. Comment claims server-side sanitisation but relies on hygiene in backend/internal/addressables/handler.go.Cross-check sanitisation rules between renderer and backend validator. Use DOMPurify as a secondary client-side guard.
app/layout.tsx47-49HighXSSTheme pack selection written to DOM via dangerouslySetInnerHTML without sanitisation. Client-side allowlist exists but could be bypassed.Use a data attribute or state-driven approach. Move the script to a Next.js Script component and load the theme via a dedicated API endpoint.
app/contexts/ScopeContext.tsx~27, ~48, ~62MediumInformation DisclosureConsole logging of grant data and permission counts in production. console.log("[ScopeContext] grants loaded", { count, grants }) exposes the user's permission matrix to browser dev tools and error monitoring services.Remove or guard all console statements behind process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'. Use structured logging (Sentry) for prod, not console.log.
app/api/dev/research/route.ts22-24MediumAccess ControlDev-only endpoint gated on NODE_ENV !== "development". NODE_ENV can be overridden by deployment tooling or misconfiguration.Require user context + permission check (permissions.DEV_*). Add a feature flag RESEARCH_ENDPOINT_ENABLED defaulting to false in prod.
app/api/dev/security-audits/route.tsSimilarMediumAccess ControlSame pattern: guards only on NODE_ENV, no auth check.Same mitigation.
app/api/dev/memory-reports/route.tsSimilarMediumAccess ControlSame pattern: guards only on NODE_ENV, no auth check.Same mitigation.
backend/cmd/server/main.go97MediumConfigurationCOOKIE_SECURE=false in dev is correct, but no warning logged if accidentally set to true in production without HTTPS. Could fail silently.If COOKIE_SECURE=true and FRONTEND_ORIGIN is not https://, log FATAL or panic. Already present for APP_ENV=production but not standalone COOKIE_SECURE.
backend/internal/security/clientip.go68LowAccess ControlX-Forwarded-For trust gated by FRONTEND_CLIENTIP_TRUSTED_CIDRS. When unset (secure default), XFF is ignored. Invalid CIDRs in loadTrustedNets() are silently skipped.Log a WARN at startup if any CIDR fails to parse. Add tests verifying the env var is read correctly.
backend/internal/ranking/service.go209LowSQL Injection (Mitigated)fmt.Sprintf("SELECT position FROM %s WHERE id = $1", table) interpolates a table name. Table comes from a registered ResourceConfig (not user input), so injection is prevented, but the pattern is fragile if the registry is not strictly validated.Document that cfg.Table must be a compile-time constant or whitelist-validated. Add a test asserting unregistered resource types are rejected.
backend/internal/auth/tokens.go79-80LowCryptographyJWT signing uses HS256 (HMAC-SHA256). If JWT_ACCESS_SECRET is weak or exposed, all tokens are compromised. No rotation implemented.Document that JWT_ACCESS_SECRET must be ≥ 32 bytes cryptographically random. Implement refresh-token rotation. Add a kid claim to support key rotation.
backend/internal/auth/password.go11LowCryptographyBcrypt cost set to 12 (modern recommendation 12-14). No rotation of cost on upgrade.Document that raising cost later still verifies old hashes; new ones use the new cost. No action needed now.
backend/internal/security/csrf.go37LowCSRF Token DesignCSRF token is 32 bytes random hex (correct). Token is readable by JS per double-submit pattern. If XSS exists, attacker can steal both cookie and header, defeating the check.Mitigate by hardening XSS defences (CSP, output encoding). Current design is standard; no change needed unless XSS risk is deemed unacceptable.
backend/internal/auth/handler.go83LowTiming AttackLogin timing equalised via equalizeResetTiming() for password-reset, but not for login itself. Wrong-password vs. non-existent-user take different wall times.Add equalizeResetTiming() to the login failure path. Trivial fix.
middleware.ts18LowAccess ControlPUBLIC_PATHS includes /v2* with comment "Phase 2 PoC: Production authz does not gate it."Link the comment to the issue/story. Verify v2 endpoints perform their own auth. Feature-flag v2 routes.
package.json60LowDependency ManagementNext.js 15.0.0, React 18.3.1 up-to-date. No obvious outdated deps.Run npm audit and npm outdated regularly. Pin to security-patch versions (e.g. ^15.1.0) to auto-patch within a release line.
backend/go.modMultipleLowDependency ManagementAll major Go deps (pgx/v5, chi/v5, jwt/v5, x/crypto) up-to-date. No vulnerable versions in require block.Run go list -u -m all regularly. Set up Dependabot or GitHub security scanning.
backend/cmd/server/main.go85-99LowConfigurationAPP_ENV must be set and validated. Good hardcodes for prod. Missing: no check that JWT secrets are sufficiently long or random.If APP_ENV is set and JWT_ACCESS_SECRET < 32 bytes, fail loudly at startup. Same for JWT_REFRESH_SECRET and MASTER_KEY.
db/mmff_vector/schema/001_init.sql40-50LowSchema Designpassword_hash is TEXT not BYTEA. Fine for bcrypt (ASCII) but unusual.No action needed. Add a CHECK constraint to validate bcrypt hash format on insert (optional).
app/contexts/AuthContext.tsxsetSessionCookieLowSession Managementsession_alive cookie with SameSite=Strict, 7-day expiry, used as UI hint. Correct design.No action needed. Document that session_alive is not an auth token.
backend/internal/logger/middleware.goN/ALowLoggingNo explicit redaction of sensitive headers (Authorization, X-CSRF-Token) from logs.Add a redaction filter for sensitive header keys.
app/lib/api.tsN/ALowData SecurityFrontend reads NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE. If misconfigured, MITM possible. No TLS pinning.Document NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE must always be https:// in prod. Consider STS preload for API subdomain.
app/api/v2/*RoutesLowAPI VersioningV2 API routes use fixture IDs instead of session context. PoC-only.Move v2 routes behind a feature flag (WORK_ITEMS_V2). Default false. Enable only in non-prod or after security review.
app/layout.tsx48LowXSS (Mitigated)Theme pack name validated against hardcoded allowlist before injection. Low risk if list is comprehensive but allowlist is in JS.Move allowlist to backend GET /api/themes. Frontend uses only server-vetted names.

Remediation Priorities

  1. Encrypt secrets in .env files (backend/.env.local, backend/.env.dev) — move to secrets manager or use encsecret encryption. Why now: hardcoded JWT secrets and DB passwords are the highest-risk compromise vector. Effort: 4-6 hours.
  2. Harden XSS defences on help documentation (Header.tsx, HelpDocRenderer.tsx) — add DOMPurify client-side and CSP nonce. Why now: backend sanitisation can fail; defence-in-depth essential. Effort: 2-3 hours.
  3. Remove or gate console.log in production (ScopeContext.tsx et al.) — wrap with NODE_ENV check or structured logger. Why now: permission matrices leaked to console + error monitoring. Effort: 1-2 hours.
  4. Add auth layer to dev endpoints (app/api/dev/*, backend/dev/*) — require user context + permission, not just NODE_ENV. Why now: NODE_ENV can be overridden. Effort: 2-3 hours.
  5. Validate env vars at startup (main.go) — check JWT secret length, MASTER_KEY entropy, COOKIE_SECURE consistency. Why now: misconfiguration could silently weaken security. Effort: 1-2 hours.
  6. Audit and rotate production secrets (.env.production.locked) — verify no prod secrets in git history; establish rotation. Why now: locked file sits on disk with no lifecycle. Effort: 3-4 hours.
  7. Document XFF trust CIDR configuration (clientip.go) — warn at startup on parse failures. Why now: misconfigurations silently allow header spoofing. Effort: 1 hour.
  8. Implement JWT rotation (tokens.go) — limit refresh token lifetime, add kid. Why now: tokens currently indefinitely reusable. Effort: 6-8 hours.
  9. Add timing defence to login endpoint (handler.go) — call equalizeResetTiming() on failed login. Why now: closes account-enumeration side channel. Effort: 30 min.
  10. Move theme pack allowlist to backend (layout.tsx) — serve from GET /api/themes. Why now: JS allowlist mutable by attacker. Effort: 2-3 hours.

Passed Checks

  • Password hashing: bcrypt cost 12 used consistently (auth/password.go). Strong default, no weak algorithms.
  • Parameterised queries: all SQL uses pgx parameterisation ($1, $2). No raw string concatenation (except whitelisted table names in ranking).
  • CSRF protection: double-submit tokens with SameSite=Strict. Exempt endpoints (login, refresh) protected by rate limiting.
  • JWT validation: HMAC-SHA256 signature verification, expiry, subject/subscription ID validation. No token leakage in errors.
  • Rate limiting: httprate middleware on sensitive endpoints (login 10/min, password-reset 3/hour, reset/confirm 10/min, general 120/min).
  • Security headers: CSP, X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, Permissions-Policy with geo/cam/mic disabled, HSTS only when HTTPS.
  • Secure cookies: refresh tokens HttpOnly=true, Secure=env(COOKIE_SECURE), SameSite=Strict. CSRF token readable by JS per double-submit pattern.
  • Error handling: RFC 9457 Problem Details format. No stack traces or DB errors leaked to clients.
  • API key security: keys prefixed sam_live_, validated server-side, subscription ID extracted from key.
  • Access control middleware: RequireAuth, RequirePermission, RequirePageAccess consistently applied. Permission resolver with 60s cache.
  • X-Forwarded-For defence: trust CIDR whitelist with secure default (empty ⇒ XFF ignored).
  • Audit logging: comprehensive audit_log table with user_id, action, resource, metadata, IP, timestamp. Append-only.
  • Database connections: pgxpool with proper cleanup. Connection strings masked in logs.
  • No dangerous crypto: no MD5, SHA1, ECB. No eval() or Function() constructors.
  • CORS configuration: AllowedOrigins from env (not *), credentials=true only with explicit origin list.
  • Frontend session management: session_alive cookie is UI hint only. Real auth via JWT + refresh cookie.
  • Dependency scanning: Next.js, React, pgx, chi, jwt/v5 all current. No obvious CVEs.
  • Secrets management pattern: .env.local and .env.dev git-ignored. .env.example documents encryption via encsecret. Pattern exists; needs adoption.
  • Production hardlock: APP_ENV=production requires COOKIE_SECURE=true and FRONTEND_ORIGIN=https://. Prevents accidental insecure prod deployments.

Coverage Notes

  • Audit focused on authentication, authorisation, data handling, and API security. Frontend rendering library security (React internals) assumed secure; XSS mitigations are application-level only.
  • SQL migrations and DB schema reviewed at high level. Row-level security (RLS) was not detected; authorisation is enforced at the application layer (permission codes, page access matrix). Acceptable for single-tenant RBAC; multi-tenant SaaS would benefit from RLS as secondary defence.
  • Email sending (SMTP) detected but endpoints not fully reviewed (password-reset link generation, XSS in emails). Assumed safe per backend/internal/messaging/email.
  • WebSocket endpoint at /ws accepts access tokens via query parameter (?access_token=...) — documented browser workaround. Safe if TOKEN_TTL is short and tokens rotate frequently.
  • Third-party integrations (Planka, Ollama, Loki) use plaintext credentials in env files. Standard for dev; ensure never used with production endpoints.
